reviews.review_msg: Make sure to attend all lectures and do the homework given to you. Most of the exam questions are based on homework assignments. The textbook is there but personally I never used it. Attend the math workshop and office hours if you need help. Overall, he is a good professor and available to help.

Took MATH 150 this spring with Vijay after failing 151 last semester with Kouzniak. Vijay is a very personable professor and made an otherwise boring class engaging. I enjoyed his stories and the life lessons he shared. He was clear about what would be on the exam and how topics would be tested and marking was lenient. Should finish with B+ or A–.

I left the class with a great understanding of the key concepts in calc 2. His lectures are useful and his pace is slow enough. Tests were fair and based on the homework questions. His accent was a little hard to understand at times.
He is funny and good at teaching. Tests are all easy. He is approachable but responds to emails very late and does not clearly answer questions and concerns students have most of the time.

Vijay is a down-to-earth guy, and definitely cracks a joke or two in lectures. I wasn't entirely fond of him writing his notes on paper, but he did spend time to rewrite them to look neater (though a bit late at times). A bit disorganized and marks are quite rigid, but he makes up for it by extending deadlines and correcting marking errors. 4.5/5

He is such a nice and easy-going guy. He is very open to questions and always finds a way to crack a joke. Although his notes were not really useful, and he writes a bit too fast and it does not help that he is left-handed when writing his paper notes, if you listen and just pay attention during lecture, you will be just fine.
Vijay is Awesome. Concepts are explained super well and always accessible for help in the course. Would highly recommend taking 152 with him.

Vijay is a great professor. He explains topics at a low level to the point that you won't come out of lectures confused. He is available out of class and provided us with sufficient material to prepare us for exams. He is generous with due dates and marking if you confront him during office hours. Don't skip class... He posts lecture notes late.

very good teacher, he goes into depth with topics and is always willing to emphasize if you don't understand something. doesn't make math unnecessarily boring or stale, and focuses on core topics without being too theoretical!
Goated prof, concepts were taught well and course was straightforward. He usually did reviews before midterms to help prepare for them. Really chill prof too.

Attendance is mandatory because he often posts his written lecture notes really late, even when the upcoming assignment is based on said notes. The weekly assignments are quite difficult and require a lot of digging around online to find answers but the midterms and the final are much easier. There are no trick questions on those tests.

I wasn't looking forward to taking MATH152 at first, as I didn't do so good in MATH150, however Vijay's lectures were extremely helpful; the exams were fairly straightforward as well. Vijay is very accessible outside of lecture, and he even made a discord server in the weeks leading up to the final exam so students could ask questions. GOATED prof

Vijay is a decent prof. He is very friendly and helpful, but he can sometimes be really careless. There are three non-cumulative midterms, and a fairly easy final as well. Honestly though, you can pretty much skip out on his lectures and do self-study or watch Jamie Mullholand videos. Not the best prof around, but he gets the job done pretty well.
Explains stuff pretty well. He made a discord server before the final and answered pretty quickly to math questions.

My love for math had slowly been dwindling. But, then Vijay started teaching me and rekindled a dying flame inside me. It was like I was watching one of those goated Indian YouTubers in real life. The dude seriously cares about his students and will go above and beyond to make sure you get what you deserve.
put him on the goat level with jamie and monogan. very fun class

He is great Math prof. His lectures are good and try to attend every lecture as it will help you so much. Grading is easy and fair.
Downside could be: Rarely responds to mails, sometimes notes not given.
Very unorganized, very easy problems in lecture but very difficult exams.
Very helpful, need more prof like him for FAN

He is a very caring professor but is uncontactable out of class, especially over email. Assignments sometimes out of nowhere, and doesn't mention them in class. He can be very disorganized with assignments and it's hard to find course materials on canvas. Mobius assignments are very very difficult and often do not relate to the midterms.

Very, very, very, very disorganized, there are assignments appear out of thin air that he never mention in class. Gradings are also ambiguous, everything is unsure, he is caring and kind, but that's all good about him, wouldn't take again for sure.

Lectures were initially synchronous, then became pre-recorded, then returned to synchronous without any formal announcements. Course itself isn't bad, but obtaining the lecture recordings, textbook materials, and lecture notes was hard because of disorganization, making the class more difficult than necessary. Khan Academy saved me in this class.

Vijay has a kind heart, however his organization could use a lot* of work. Very often the lectures would be pushed back, we would be given assignments before we learned the material, and he is very unresponsive outside of class time. No office hours. I self taught almost the whole course using the textbook (which is not mandatory). Unneeded stress.

-Vijay is confusing, dysfunctional and messy + his caring attitude can't carry the course, ur on ur own -not a lot of practice is given, *READ THE TEXTBOOK*, do exercises inside -all assignments very easy, unit tests not hard but unpredictable so tell Vijay what you're uncomfortable with (he's responsive in class but unresponsive w/ email)

Vijay is a pretty solid math professor. The class was online and lectures pre-recorded. The pre-recorded lectures were OK, but if you went to his zoom office hours, he always went over the most important things and explained them very well. Before the final he had office hours everyday, even on the weekend. All in all, a very helpful professor.

Office hours were worth it, he even had a discord channel during the end. Video lectures are not well modulated though, he need to work on that. Otherwise, super helpful prof, make sure to attend office hours.

Probably the worst professor I've had in post-secondary so far. His prerecorded lectures are outdated, disorganized and confusing. He doesn't seem very concerned with students understanding the concept, vaguely re-explaining when asked. You don't want this guy for a whole semester, save yourself the trouble and watch Jamie Mulhollands videos insted

Definitely one of the best profs out there! Had him for calc 2 last sem and have used his calc 3 videos this semester. Lectures can feel a bit disorganized but nonetheless he explains things quite well. Exams in calc II were fair, some very hard questions but that's to be expected in calc 2. Extremely helpful during office hours and a nice guy!

Dr. singh tries to be a good prof, but he just isn't a good lecturer. His lectures are very scattered, and difficult to follow because a lot of the time he would accidentally skip something and goes back and talks about it. Poor organization of the course, very little communication. I've done very well on the course, but everything was self learned

Disliked lecture hours being used as office hours- forces lectures to be watched on your own time and attend office hours, or miss the opportunity to ask questions. Not conducive to learning. Some exams were extremely unrealistic and others were blatant copy-paste jobs of the provided materials. Class essentially ended up being self-learned.

The first midterm was quite easy but the second midterm and final were hard. Mulholland's pre-recorded lectures are much more useful. Vijay's class and videos are a mess. Half the time he does not correctly answer student's questions. He does not respond to emails. I did not learn a single thing from him all semester. Be aware of trick questions.

His lecture videos are not as well made as those made by other prof teaching the same course at SFU. He didn't put much effort into them. The difficulty of tests is inconsistent. One MT and one part of the final is almost too easy and the other MT and the other part of the final is hard. You have short quizzes to do every week as well.

HE does not update the recorded lecture in time, which leads me have to study myself to finish assignments. Also, near the end of the semester, he did not update the lectures from class; instead, he used the video from the past which is inconsistent with the lecture notes. And he never rely your emails...
Great guy! The first midterm was too easy, but the second one was totally fair. He really seems to enjoy teaching and is always happy to explain things. Great prof, highly recommend!!

Tough course but he explains the topics well, although I have previous integration experience. Lectures were recorded and lecture slots were used for office hours. Explains examples clearly. Kind of seems annoyed by students asking questions but I think that's just how he speaks. Midterm was fair.

Such a nice prof always encouraged questions and never made you feel dumb, always inspired me and made course content much more enjoyable midterms did feel rushed but the exam was very fair but he did post online lectures pretty late sometimes

I really enjoyed his class. The material was (for the most part) delivered very clearly and it is very easy to ask him questions in office hours if there is something you dont understand. He even made a class discord before the final so we could ask any last minute questions. Super great guy who wants you to learn as long as you are willing to try
He is a VERY unorganized guy.

I found Dr. Singh to be accommodating to the shift to online learning. Throughout the semester he would listen to the class when it came to how we felt the course was being delivered. Overall a pretty solid prof!

Lectures are mostly clear and thorough; although he sometimes jumps between points, he makes up for it by being super approachable and helpful. During office hours he always makes sure the student understands his answer before moving on, never scolds students for asking "dumb" questions like some profs, and listens to feedback. Overall a great guy

His video lectures are unclear and his handwriting sucks which makes learning from his lectures very hard, I had to result to watching another persons lectures as they were way more helpful. Doesn't email back unless you spam his inbox (which I had to do several times to get a response), his class site is also a mess and hard to navigate.

This is my 4th class with Vijay. Vijay cares about his students, and his exams are fair. If you go to him for help, he will make sure you understand; unfortunately, if you dont, you will likely find yourself confused, as his lecture notes can sometimes be scattered. His mic also had issues throughout, so hopefully that gets fixed moving forward.

He's an example of what a truly caring person is. You can also see his strong appreciation for math in his lectures - he's concerned with using his knowledge in meaningful ways. He teaches other classes and manages workshops by himself, so something might get lost in his big inbox. Work with him and he'll work with you; one of the best profs here!

Be prepared to never get email responses back. 3 times now I've had to email him, the TA, and then finally the math undergrad adviser to remind Vijay just to get back to me. Grades tough but overall is clear and concise with his lectures. No problem with his teaching just everything else about him.
vijay is one the best math prof at SFU. At this point of pandemic he is more helpful than many of other professors. go vijay go!
Had him last term, was of no help at all. Never replied to emails, and really terrible instruction with homework and teaching. Do not take it with him.

I am also currently in Vijay's class. He is a fantastic professor and his jokes are quite enjoyable. There's no way to make the math easy, however, Vijay makes the lecture fun. The person who was complaining about his jokes in class is quite unaware of how some other professors can be. Great class, great prof, would highly recommend to anyone.

taking his class right now. REALLY BAD LECTURER. MAKES JOKES THAT ARE NOT EVEN FUNNY. THE WAY HE LECTURES IS VERY CONFUSING BECAUSE HE IS CHANGING TOPICS IN BETWEEN OTHER TOPICS. HE MAKES SEVERAL MISTAKES WHILE LECTURING AND SAYS " IF YOU DO THAT IN THE EXAM YOU WILL LOSE MARKS, BUT I WONT BECAUSE IM THE ONE TEACHING HAHA" REALLY BAD PROF!!!!!

Great guy, genuinely wants students to do well. Very easy to reach outside of class, and helpful. He has lots of energy during lectures, which can make him hard to follow at times, but he is always happy to clarify and discuss things further if you miss something. Overall a wonderful teacher/person who will go out of his way to help if you ask :)

Vijay is a solid choice for Macm316. Overall, the course was fair and deadlines were extended when needed. If your bad at math, I recommend you take no more than 2 other courses or 1 if you're really struggling atm. Quizzes were similar to hwk. Try your best on Quizzes + Computer assignments because they are the best way to earn marks (Worth 40%)!

Vijay is an authentic person. He held office hours on Saturday before our final. He's very fair with marking, and gives you enough time to do assignments. He does his best to make sure you understand. He's friendly, funny, caring and inspirational. Sometimes his writing is cluttered but he'll explain it to you if you ask. So happy he's at SFU.

He made MACM316 tolerable for me. He is very reasonable in terms of assignment due dates (he would extend assignment due dates when students are struggling), marking is fair as well. His tutorials are extremely helpful so you better not miss them unless you really really can't make it. Would strongly recommend for MACM316!

hE explains things very clearly. he is definiely one of the best profs in SFU's math department. Everything he goes over in the lecture and review is important. He prepares us very well for all exams, so there are no suprprises

Highly suggest Professor Vijay for this math course. I really enjoyed the course through his teachings, which were clear, concise and well directed. He would do his best at making seemingly tough problems very understandable. With this, participation, attendance and being prepared prior to class is very necessary.

I really enjoyed this prof and could tell he wanted the best for his students even if the class was pretty hard. You get test grades back the next day which is amazing, but you really need to put time into this class to pass. All the difficulties came from the concepts themselves not the prof.

I took vijay at Langara college for math1152. He is a VERY unorganized guy. He forgot we had quizzes multiply times. He takes days to reply to emails & if you speak with him in person he apologizes then forgets to do what you asked for. HE MESSED UP PROBLEMS EVERY CLASS. He doesn't even know what he's teaching and the students would correct him.
the best calculus teacher i took math152 with him again

MM100 is a tutorial class for MATH100 . The first class he did not show up. In the second class duration of 1hour, he came after 35 minutes. Stays in his own world and tries to make students leave class as soon as he can. not very helpful as one should expect from a tutorial class. Bad selection of prof. :(

great prof. makes concepts easy and a very fair marker, lenient, and hilarious. Best math class, and vijay makes the class interesting and he has your attention all class. Exams and quizzes were straightforward.
Highly recommended!
simplyy the bestt..!!!!!!!!!!
The professor is very interactive and helps out very often. Keeps everyone on there feet to learn and is a brilliant grader.

He's such a good Math professor. Sometimes he makes very tiny mistakes in lecture notes, but that's fine because he is a super busy and hardworking professor. You will find your way enjoying his class anyways.
To be honest, I have no idea how he marks the in-class quizzes and the exams. He just keeps marking my papers wrong again and again.

By no means is this class easy but taking it with Vijay really made it a pleasant experience. You can tell he wants everyone to succeed because he goes out of his way to provide help when you ask for it. This class does require you to spend time doing the assignments because that's what you're tested on and that's how you get the best grade.

Vijay was a hilarious professor and always had the class laughing. He is easy going and was always willing to help students if you needed him. He was unorganized with homework assignments but besides that I would recommend him to everyone!
Easiest class I've taken. Thought Math157 would be hard, but ended up being a GPA booster.

Vijay's the man. He is enthusiastic in the work he does and he is willing to work with students if they have concerns. It is really fun attending his lectures and he is one of the most easy going guys.
Great prof.  Weekly quizzes got confusing near the end of semester. Midterms and final was fair. Bonus questions helped a lot. Do all the assigned questions and youll do fine.

Easy A, take it with him if you want a GPA booster. Teaches right out of the textbook, didn't show up at least 80% of the classes and aced all his midterms and final. Is a nice man and genuinely care about his students and how well they do. Average for midterm were both around 60 ish and the final average was 55.
Best Prof ever!
I LOVE HIM. BEST PROF EVER. 75% = A-,60% = B-. BEST PROF EVER.
NO! Really made it difficult to succeed in an already difficult class. Not very helpful. A nice guy, but not a great teacher. I recommend taking this class with someone else if at all possible.

he goes over very basic examples in class and seems very unprepared. Sometimes he looks at the textbook and just wings the section based off the first example in the text or he'll make the simplest thing he can make to finish it quickly. Definitely needs to put more time in preparing his lectures.

For the first half of the semester, it seems as if he just took the first boat out of india to teach math at SFU. His accent is hard to understand at times, makes frequent mistakes in class, breezes through important material. But recently, he has been showing more effort by giving us notes. Nice guy at heart but being a professor is not his forte

The first and second midsem were very fair as it was mostly online questions and instructor assignments. Review class is a definite boost for exams. He is helpful in person.The big drawback is that he doesnt puts the  notes online.

I agree with the first person who commented.  He puts down anyone who asks questions and never really answers them when asked. He is unprepared to class and when he writes a hard question he just ends up skipping over it.  He covers very little or only the very surface of concepts being tested on and consistently makes mistakes in his classes.
Dr. Singh is a very good teacher. He is very helpful and gives extra time at the end of the class for questions. But he doesn't gives notes.
